Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
M. Perry Chapman papers
Chapman, M. Perry.
8453
27 cubic feet.
2 mapcase folders.
1963-2010. Professional records, reports, architectural plans, and publications. Collection consists of M. Perry Chapman's professional records. Includes project files, reports, master plans, and publications related to campus planning. Collection material in English 8512061
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
December 2014
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, December 2014
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, October 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
M. Perry Chapman was born in Cooperstown, New York and graduated from Cornell University with a bachelor of architecture degree in 1960. After working for the city planning bureau in Rochester, New York, he joined Sasaki Associates in Watertown, Massachusetts in 1964, where he worked for the reminder of his career. His early projects were master plans and environmental impact studies for large projects such as the 1980 Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, New York and the 1984 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles. He then headed the firm's campus planning practice and worked with dozens of universities across the United States, focusing in this area for most of his career. He was also a member of the Belmont, Massachusetts Planning Board and the Metropolitan Area Planning Council. He wrote and spoke frequently on the theory and practice of planning, publishing his book "American Places: In Search of the Twenty-First Century Campus" in 2006. That year, the American Planning Association's New England Chapter awarded him their Distinguished Achievement Award, and he received the K.C. Parsons Founder's Award for Distinguished Achievement in Higher Education Planning from the Society for College and University Planning in 2008. The Society for College and University Planning and the Hideo Sasaki Foundation award the annual Perry Chapman Prize in his honor.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection consists of M. Perry Chapman's professional records. Includes project files, reports, master plans, and publications related to campus planning.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Colleges and Universities Boxes 1-24, Mapcase Folder 1
Alabama - Georgia Illinois - New York North Carolina - Wyoming; Canada and Other Non-U.S. Campuses Business School Related
Series II. Olympic Games Boxes 3, 8, 14
Series III. City Planning Boxes 2-3, 6-11, 13-14, 17, 20, 24, Mapcase Folder 1
Series IV. General Planning and Administrative Files Boxes 12, 19-21, 25-27
Series V. Media and Memorabilia Boxes 6, 28 and Mapcase Folder 2
Plans - Digital Files Presentations and Papers - Digital Files Memorabilia
Cite As:
M. Perry Chapman Papers, #8453. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
